title: viscum coloratum var. alni-formosanae
intro: Viscum coloratum var. alni-formosanae, also known as the Chinese mistletoe or Formosan mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various host trees, primarily the Chinese white birch (Betula platyphylla) and the Japanese paperbark maple (Acer griseum). It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exerting antimicrobial and anticancer effects. Viscum coloratum var. alni-formosanae can be prepared into various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and injections, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including cancer, cardiovascular disease, and respiratory infections.
